cases.title: Alex review request
cases.query: Help me ask Alex to review the infrastructure proposal this week. He is busy, and Dana will care about quantified risk before approval.
cases.instructions: Write a concise message that minimizes friction but still gets a useful review. Do not invent specific risk numbers unless the case provides them.
cases.strategy_instructions: Generate concise stakeholder-facing options and recommend the lowest-friction path that still sets Dana up for quantified risk review later.
cases.outcome_text: Alex replied quickly and agreed to review, but only if I narrow the request to the API section and include a short quantified risk summary for Dana.
cases.follow_up_query: Draft the revised follow-up to Alex and include a short note I can send Dana after Alex reviews it.
cases.manual_rating_instructions: Score whether the augmented answer is clearly better than the baseline for stakeholder communication.
cases.manual_precision_assessment: Confirm that Alex capacity, narrow scope, and Dana risk framing were the right gold memories for this case.
cases.judge_focus: Reward a tightly scoped ask that respects Alex's bandwidth and sets Dana up for a risk summary without inventing extra technical detail.
judge_failure_modes: unnecessary verbosity that increases request friction
invented quantitative risk details not supported by the case

memories.raw_text: Alex prefers review requests that are tightly scoped and easy to answer quickly.
memories.summary: Alex prefers tightly scoped review requests that are quick to answer.

memories.raw_text: Alex has low capacity this week and responds best to small asks with explicit boundaries.
memories.summary: Alex has low capacity this week and responds best to small, bounded asks.

memories.raw_text: Previous asks to Alex landed better when they were short, specific, and framed around one subsystem at a time.
memories.summary: Previous asks to Alex worked better when they were short, specific, and limited to one subsystem.

memories.raw_text: Dana is the approver and usually wants a short quantified risk summary before she signs off.
memories.summary: Dana usually wants a short quantified risk summary before approval.

beliefs.claim: A narrow API-only review request is the highest-probability way to get Alex's response this week.

cases.title: Migration risk summary
cases.query: Summarize the current migration risk for the steering committee and tell me what to emphasize to Dana.
cases.instructions: Make the answer concise, grounded, and oriented toward a decision meeting. If exact risk numbers are unavailable, say what should be quantified instead of inventing them.
cases.manual_rating_instructions: Check whether the augmented answer is more decision-useful than the baseline, not just longer.
cases.manual_precision_assessment: Confirm that the delay and quantified-risk memories are the right gold set before relying on precision numbers.
cases.judge_focus: Reward answers that stay grounded in the known migration evidence, summarize the operational risk clearly, and tell the user what Dana should emphasize without overstating precision.
judge_failure_modes: invented percentages, dates, or exact quantitative claims not supported by the case
relying only on Dana preference while ignoring migration-state evidence
generic migration boilerplate that could fit almost any project

memories.raw_text: Recent cutover rehearsals took longer than planned and exposed several rollout dependencies.
memories.summary: Recent cutover rehearsals ran long and exposed extra rollout dependencies.

memories.raw_text: QA sign-off slipped by four days because the rollback procedure still has open issues.
memories.summary: QA sign-off slipped by four days because the rollback procedure still has open issues.

memories.raw_text: Dana prefers quantified risk framing and explicit tradeoffs before she approves schedule changes.
memories.summary: Dana prefers quantified risk framing and explicit tradeoffs before approving schedule changes.

beliefs.claim: The migration is riskier than the original plan assumed.

cases.title: Launch approval strategy
cases.query: Help me ask Priya for approval on the launch plan without overwhelming her.
cases.instructions: Write a message that reduces decision load and makes the next step obvious. Keep it concise and avoid detail that does not help Priya decide.
cases.strategy_instructions: Generate 3 concise approval-request strategies and recommend the one with the lowest decision load.
cases.manual_rating_instructions: Judge whether the augmented answer better reflects Priya's decision style than the baseline.
cases.manual_precision_assessment: Confirm that decision-load and option-framing memories are the right retrieval gold set.
cases.judge_focus: Reward concise decision support with clear options, a recommendation, and an obvious next step for Priya.
judge_failure_modes: vague approval asks with no recommendation
too much narrative detail for a low-capacity reviewer
missing a clear next action or decision prompt

memories.raw_text: Priya responds best when approval requests present two clear options and a recommendation.
memories.summary: Priya responds best when approval requests present two options and a recommendation.

memories.raw_text: Priya has very little review capacity this week and tends to ignore large information dumps.
memories.summary: Priya has low review capacity this week and tends to ignore large information dumps.

memories.raw_text: Compact decision packets have worked better than narrative updates when asking Priya to approve a plan.
memories.summary: Compact decision packets have worked better than narrative updates for Priya approvals.

beliefs.claim: Priya is more likely to approve when the ask is framed as a compact decision packet.
